ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Evidentiary Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Continued To Jun-09-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PES-23-306871 The Estate of Nabiha Dabit and PTR25-308070 In the Matter of Nabiha Dabit 2016 Revocable Trust.

Appearances:
John Lewman, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for Shadia Dabit, Petitioner.
Mark Kelly, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Shafi Dabit, Objector.
David Monsour, Esq., in court, for and with Joseph Dabit, Petitioner.
Renda Dabit, appeared in person, in propria persona, interested party.

Councils presented oral arguments to support their position regarding the petitions before the Court. Discussion was held regarding the deeds sent by Ms. Young, that The deeds will need to be recorded, and that it should be done within 30 days.

First account and report of trustee is continued to August 18, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court also orders parties to file the petition by June 16, 2025, that the response be filed by July 21, 2025, and that the reply to be filed by August 7, 2025.

Status of evidentiary hearing, Petition for probate of will and letters for testamentary, and Case management conference are continued to June 9, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court ordered extension of special letters to the next hearing date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Conference
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Continued To Jun-09-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-27-2025 Continued To Apr-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
John Lewman, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Shadia Dabit, Petitioner.
Mark Kelly, Esq., via Zoom videoconference, for and with Shafi Dabit, Objector.
David Monsour, Esq., in court, for and with Joseph Dabit, Petitioner.
Renda Dabit, via Zoom videoconference, in propria persona, interested party.

At the outset, counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the competing petitions before the court. There is discussion regarding whether an evidentiary hearing is being requested. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding a possible evidentiary hearing. The court advises counsel that it typically does not set an evidentiary hearing until it issues a Case Management Order. The court's intent is discussed.

At the conclusion of discussion, the subject matters are continued to Apr-28-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The court is to issue a Case Management Order. A Case Management Hearing and a Status of Evidentiary Hearing are set for Apr-28-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Letters of Special Administration are extended and shall now expire on Apr-28-2025. Counsel for Joseph Dabit submits a proposed order thereon.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-05-2024 Continued To Nov-18-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Mansour for and with Joseph Dabit.
John Lewman appeared by zoom for and with Shadia Dabit by zoom.
Mark Kelly appeared by zoom for and with Shafy Dabit by zoom.
Renda Dabit in propria person.

Mr. Mansour stated that an estate account is open, that rent is going into the account and expenses are being paid, that the Piedmont property is being rented and that both of the properties are insured. Mr. Mansour also stated that there is $8,200.00 in the estate account right now, that his client would like a continuance to work things out, that a trust accounting has been provided, that he was hoping to get a demand and respond to the demand, requested 100 days and stated that if there is no resolution, the matter could be set for trial and that he will be seeking a global resolution. The court spoke of its familiarity with the family dynamics, its usual suggestion of having a professional fiduciary, and stated that it thinks this probate will be opened and closed rather quickly as the estate property will be moved into the trust. Renda, Mr. Lewman and Mr. Kelly presented oral argument to support their position with respect to the removal of Joseph and the appointment of a third-party professional. Discussion was held regarding the amounts of money that is being funneled to Joseph. The court encourages the parties not to expend money on an evidentiary hearing, to try and negotiate this and reminds the parties of its authority to issue a Breslin order. The Amended Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary and the Petition for Probate of Will and Letters of Administration with Will Annexed are continued to November 18, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The special letters of administration are also extended to November 18, 2024. The court encouraged the parties to schedule mediation before the next hearing date and encouraged Joseph to provide documents.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

Off Calendar Apr-15-2024 Continued To Aug-05-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Mansour for and with Joseph Dabit.
John Lewman appeared by zoom for and with Shadia Dabit by zoom.
Mark Kelly appeared by zoom for and with Shafy Dabit by zoom.
Renda Dabit in propria person.

The court noted that written objections were recently filed. Discussion was held regarding Joseph having been named as the executor by his mother and also being the trustee, Joseph's intent to provide an accounting in about a month, three of the units in the Oakland property being rented out, Joseph currently staying in the Piedmont house to prevent squatters, but having a renter ready, Renda's support of Joseph and wanting to get the property sold and get this done, family members concern about the money that Joseph has taken, the request to have the accounting go through March, no one having petitioned for the appointment of a professional fiduciary, the trust not being under court supervision, the issue of having a professional being expensive and how they would be paid (from the trust or the estate), the request to have more time to do discovery, the issue of Joseph coming in at a limited capacity, the issue of having insurance on the property, etc. The court encourages the parties to agree on getting all of the properties rented. Mr. Lewman stated that he filed a competing petition on April 5th. The clerk informed Mr. Lewman that no such petition is pending in the e-filing queue. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to August 5, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
